transcript.speaker: therapist
transcript.content: I'm glad to be here with you, Linda. I wonder if we could explore the idea of feeling humiliated or embarrassed in certain situations. transcript.speaker: client
transcript.content: Linda: I guess, I mean, it’s not like I walk around thinking about it all the time. transcript.speaker: therapist

transcript.speaker: therapist

The questions helped the client articulate their emotions and thoughts more clearly, leading to a better understanding of their internal struggles. However, while the questions were effective in prompting exploration, they did not fully challenge the client's existing beliefs or introduce completely new perspectives, which is why the rating is not higher.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: I guess, I mean, it’s not like I walk around thinking about it all the time. But yeah, I do feel embarrassed sometimes, especially when my family brings up my job situation. It’s like they don’t get how hard it is for me. They just assume I should be doing better, and it makes me feel... I don’t know, like I’m not good enough or something. But, I mean, who really wants to talk about that? It’s just frustrating.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: I mean, I guess there’s some truth to that. I’ve had my share of feeling rejected, especially when it comes to jobs. It’s like I put myself out there, and then I get shut down, and it just reinforces that feeling of not being good enough. And in personal relationships, it can feel similar. I try to connect with people, but sometimes it just doesn’t click, and I end up feeling isolated. It’s frustrating because I want to be open and make those connections, but then I worry about getting hurt or rejected again. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of wanting more but being afraid to reach for it.

I worry that if I try to step up or make connections, I’ll just end up failing again. It’s like I’m in this weird limbo where I want to grow, but I’m also terrified of what that might mean for me. I guess it’s just hard to reconcile wanting to be better with the fear of falling flat on my face.

retrieval_events.query: Client: Linda: Yeah, I think that definitely resonates. My living situation feels like it’s always there, looming over me. It’s like I can’t escape that pressure, and it just feeds into everything else. When I think about trying to make changes in my career or even just in my life, I feel like I have this constant reminder of instability at home. It’s hard to feel like I can take risks when I’m just trying to keep everything together in my personal life. Sometimes I wonder if I’d feel more confident if my living situation was more settled, but then I also worry that I’m just making excuses for not pushing myself. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop of wanting to change but feeling trapped by my circumstances.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: Yeah, that definitely resonates. I feel like I’m constantly trying to regain some control over my life, but it’s like every time I think I’m making progress, something else comes up to knock me back down. It’s frustrating because I want to take charge, but then I get overwhelmed by everything that feels out of my hands. I mean, I know I can’t control everything, but it just feels like I’m at the mercy of my circumstances, and that’s a tough pill to swallow. I guess I just want to feel like I have some say in my own life, you know? But then I get stuck in this cycle of feeling powerless, and it just makes me want to retreat even more. It’s exhausting trying to balance that desire for control with the reality of what’s happening around me.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: Yeah, that definitely resonates. I mean, I often feel like I’m just not cut out for handling challenges, you know? It’s like I look at others who seem to manage everything so effortlessly, and I can’t help but think, “Why can’t I be like that?” It just reinforces this idea that I’m somehow less capable. I guess I’ve built up this narrative in my head that I’m not competent enough to tackle anything significant. And when I face challenges, it feels like I’m just waiting for the moment when I’ll prove that narrative right. It’s frustrating because I know I want to be better, but that inner voice just keeps telling me I’m not. It’s like a constant battle between wanting to believe in myself and feeling like I’m destined to fail.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: I guess when I think about it, a lot of it probably comes from my upbringing. My parents always had high expectations, and it felt like I was never quite meeting them. I remember times when I tried really hard to do well in school or other activities, but it seemed like no matter what I did, it was never enough. They would praise my siblings for their achievements, and I just felt like I was in the background, you know? It’s like I internalized this idea that if I wasn’t excelling, then I was failing. And now, it’s like I can’t shake that feeling off. It’s hard to break free from that mindset when it feels so ingrained in me. I just keep thinking that maybe if I had done better back then, I wouldn’t be in this position now. It’s frustrating to feel like I’m still living in that shadow, and it makes it hard to see a way out.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: I think those experiences really make me hesitant to take risks now. It’s like I have this fear of failing again, and I can’t shake off the feeling that if I try and don’t succeed, it’ll just confirm everything I’ve always believed about myself. I keep thinking about how I felt when I didn’t meet my parents’ expectations, and it just makes me want to avoid putting myself in situations where I could be judged or criticized. I mean, it’s exhausting to constantly feel like I have to prove myself, and the thought of facing that kind of disappointment again is just too much to handle. So, I end up holding back, even when I know I should be pushing myself. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of fear and self-doubt, and it’s really hard to break free from it.

retrieval_events.query: Client: Linda: When I think about taking a risk, even a small one, I start to feel this knot in my stomach. It’s like a mix of anxiety and dread. I think, “What if I fail? What if I embarrass myself?” Those thoughts just flood in, and it makes me want to retreat. I mean, I know I should be pushing myself, but then I get caught up in this cycle of overthinking everything. I start imagining all the ways it could go wrong, and it feels so overwhelming. It’s frustrating because I want to be brave and take those steps, but that fear just holds me back. I keep telling myself that maybe it’s safer to stay where I am, even if it’s not where I want to be. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to move forward and being paralyzed by the fear of what might happen.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: Yeah, definitely. When those fears creep in, I find myself thinking things like, “I’m just not cut out for this,” or “I’ll never be good enough.” It’s like I can hear that inner voice reminding me of all the times I’ve fallen short. I start to believe that if I do fail, it’ll just prove that I’m a disappointment, not just to myself but to everyone around me. I mean, I know it’s not rational, but in those moments, it feels so real. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t see any other possibility except for failure. And then I just end up avoiding the situation altogether, which only reinforces that belief that I can’t handle things. It’s frustrating because I want to break free from that, but it feels like I’m fighting against something that’s been part of me for so long.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: Well, when those fears become overwhelming, I tend to just shut down. I mean, I avoid the situation altogether, like I mentioned before. It’s like my brain goes into this protective mode where I just want to hide from everything. I might distract myself with something else, like binge-watching a show or scrolling through my phone, just to escape those feelings. But deep down, I know it’s not really helping. It’s more like a temporary fix, you know? I just keep pushing those feelings aside, thinking maybe they’ll go away on their own. But then, they just keep piling up, and I end up feeling even worse. It’s frustrating because I know I should be facing things head-on, but it’s like I can’t muster the energy to do it. I feel trapped in this cycle of avoidance, and it’s exhausting.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: You know, I think a big part of it is just the fear of judgment. I mean, when I think about facing a challenge, I can’t help but worry about what others will think if I fail. It feels like there’s this spotlight on me, and I’m just waiting for someone to point out my mistakes or shortcomings. It’s like I’ve internalized this idea that I have to be perfect, and anything less than that is unacceptable. So, when I feel that pressure, I just want to escape. It’s easier to hide than to risk being seen as a failure. And then there’s this nagging feeling that if I do fail, it’ll just confirm all those negative beliefs I have about myself. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where the thought of facing reality feels too heavy, and I just want to retreat to a place where I don’t have to deal with it. But then, of course, that just makes everything worse in the long run. It’s frustrating, really.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: Yeah, I can think of a few moments that really stand out. Like, there was this one time in high school when I was working on a big project, and I thought I had put in a lot of effort. But when I got my grade back, it was way lower than I expected. I remember feeling so embarrassed in front of my classmates, and I could just feel their eyes on me, judging me. My teacher even made a comment about how I should have done better, and it felt like everyone was just waiting for me to fail. That really stuck with me. It was like a confirmation of all those fears I had about not being good enough. Ever since then, I’ve been terrified of putting myself out there, worried that I’ll just end up disappointing everyone again. It’s like that moment replayed in my mind over and over, and it just solidified this idea that I have to be perfect or else I’ll face that kind of humiliation again. It’s exhausting to carry that around.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: Honestly, it brings up a mix of shame and anger. I feel ashamed that I let that moment define so much of how I see myself now. I mean, it was just one grade, but it felt like the end of the world at the time. And then there's this anger, not just at the teacher for their comment, but also at myself for letting it affect me so deeply. I wish I could just shake it off and not let it control me like this. It’s frustrating to think that something from so long ago still has such a grip on me. I want to move past it, but it feels like every time I try to take a step forward, that memory pulls me back, reminding me of my failures. It’s like I’m stuck in this tug-of-war between wanting to let go and being held back by that fear of judgment.

retrieval_events.query: Linda: You know, I’ve thought about that a lot. Letting go of that memory would mean freeing myself from this constant pressure to be perfect. It would be like giving myself permission to make mistakes and not have it define who I am. I think it would feel like a weight being lifted off my shoulders, but at the same time, it’s scary to imagine. I mean, what if I let go and then still fail? It’s like I’m caught in this paradox where I want to be free, but I’m terrified of what that freedom might look like. I guess it would mean learning to accept that I’m not perfect and that it’s okay to just be me, flaws and all. But then I wonder, can I really do that? It feels so daunting, and I’m not sure where to even start.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Humiliation (Trauma)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: I guess, I mean, it’s not like I walk around thinking about it all the time. But yeah, I do feel embarrassed sometimes, especially when my family brings up my job situation. It’s like they don’t get how hard it is for me. They just assume I should be doing better, and it makes me feel... I don’t know, like I’m not good enough or something. But, I mean, who really wants to talk about that? It’s just frustrating.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Significant loss (Loss)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: I guess there is some of that, yeah. I mean, I had this idea of where I’d be by now, you know? Like, I thought I’d be in a job I actually liked, maybe even doing something meaningful. But instead, I feel stuck. It’s like I’m just going through the motions, and that’s not what I wanted for myself. But then I think, maybe I’m just not cut out for anything better. I don’t know if that makes sense. It’s just frustrating to think about what could have been, and then to have them bring it up, it’s like... I don’t know, it just hits a nerve.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Domestic instability (Housing/Home)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: I mean, yeah, I guess there’s some instability there too. It’s not just about the job, you know? It’s like everything feels a bit up in the air. I’m not in a place I really want to be, and that adds to the pressure. I think about how I’m supposed to be figuring out my career while also dealing with where I live and all that. It’s just... overwhelming. Sometimes I feel like I’m juggling too many things at once, and I’m afraid I’m going to drop them all. It’s hard to focus on one thing when everything feels so uncertain.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Relationship rejection (Relationships)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: I mean, I guess there’s some truth to that. I’ve had my share of feeling rejected, especially when it comes to jobs. It’s like I put myself out there, and then I get shut down, and it just reinforces that feeling of not being good enough. And in personal relationships, it can feel similar. I try to connect with people, but sometimes it just doesn’t click, and I end up feeling isolated. It’s frustrating because I want to be open and make those connections, but then I worry about getting hurt or rejected again. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of wanting more but being afraid to reach for it.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Role insecurity (Work)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: Well, yeah, I think there’s definitely some insecurity about my role at work. I mean, when I think about it, I don’t really feel like I have a solid footing there. It’s like I’m just trying to keep my head above water, and that makes it hard to feel confident in reaching out to others or even taking on new challenges. I worry that if I try to step up or make connections, I’ll just end up failing again. It’s like I’m in this weird limbo where I want to grow, but I’m also terrified of what that might mean for me. I guess it’s just hard to reconcile wanting to be better with the fear of falling flat on my face.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Living arrangement tension (Housing/Home)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Client: Linda: Yeah, I think that definitely resonates. My living situation feels like it’s always there, looming over me. It’s like I can’t escape that pressure, and it just feeds into everything else. When I think about trying to make changes in my career or even just in my life, I feel like I have this constant reminder of instability at home. It’s hard to feel like I can take risks when I’m just trying to keep everything together in my personal life. Sometimes I wonder if I’d feel more confident if my living situation was more settled, but then I also worry that I’m just making excuses for not pushing myself. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop of wanting to change but feeling trapped by my circumstances.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Loss of control (Safety)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: Yeah, that definitely resonates. I feel like I’m constantly trying to regain some control over my life, but it’s like every time I think I’m making progress, something else comes up to knock me back down. It’s frustrating because I want to take charge, but then I get overwhelmed by everything that feels out of my hands. I mean, I know I can’t control everything, but it just feels like I’m at the mercy of my circumstances, and that’s a tough pill to swallow. I guess I just want to feel like I have some say in my own life, you know? But then I get stuck in this cycle of feeling powerless, and it just makes me want to retreat even more. It’s exhausting trying to balance that desire for control with the reality of what’s happening around me.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Competence concern (Identity)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Linda: Yeah, that definitely resonates. I mean, I often feel like I’m just not cut out for handling challenges, you know? It’s like I look at others who seem to manage everything so effortlessly, and I can’t help but think, “Why can’t I be like that?” It just reinforces this idea that I’m somehow less capable. I guess I’ve built up this narrative in my head that I’m not competent enough to tackle anything significant. And when I face challenges, it feels like I’m just waiting for the moment when I’ll prove that narrative right. It’s frustrating because I know I want to be better, but that inner voice just keeps telling me I’m not. It’s like a constant battle between wanting to believe in myself and feeling like I’m destined to fail.
